lines: Welcome. [long pause] Tonight is different from the other nights. [long pause] We're not going to build an elaborate scene. [long pause] We're not going to work through the body piece by piece in great detail. [long pause] Tonight, we're going to do something that sounds simple and turns out to be one of the hardest things a human being can do.
But first, the body needs to arrive. [long pause] Even stillness needs a foundation. [long pause] So we start there.

Adjust your position. [long pause] Move slowly. [long pause] Find where your body wants to be tonight. [long pause] Let your head settle into the pillow... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] let your arms fall wherever they fall... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] let your legs be heavy and slightly apart. [long pause] If you need to shift your hips or roll your shoulders, do that now. [long pause] Take your time. [long pause] There's no one waiting for you to be ready.

Now notice the surface beneath you. [long pause] The mattress. [long pause] The sheets. [long pause] The way the bed receives your full weight without complaint. [long pause] It has been doing this every night for years, and it has never once failed at the job. [long pause] You are supported. [long pause] Physically, tangibly, right now. [long pause] Supported.

Bring your awareness to your feet. [long pause] Don't move them. [long pause] Don't tense them. [long pause] Just notice them. [long pause] The temperature... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the weight... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] whether they feel heavy or restless. [long pause] The tops of the feet, the soles, the toes. [long pause] Just visiting. [long pause] Just acknowledging that they're there, at the far end of your body, done with the day.

Now your ankles. [long pause] Your shins. [long pause] Your calves. [long pause] Feel the muscle and bone resting against the bed. [long pause] Whether they're tight or loose, warm or cool... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] it doesn't matter. [long pause] You're not trying to change anything. [long pause] You're just moving your awareness through, the way you'd walk slowly through a house at night, touching each doorframe as you pass.

Your knees. [long pause] Your thighs, the largest muscles in your body, pressing down into the mattress. [long pause] Heavy with the accumulated effort of standing, walking, climbing stairs, shifting weight from foot to foot. [long pause] All of that is done. [long pause] Let the thighs rest in their full heaviness.

Your hips and your pelvis. [long pause] The bowl of the body. [long pause] The center of gravity. [long pause] Feel the weight pooling here, settling deep, anchoring you to the bed. [long pause] Your lower back, arched or flat, the spine doing whatever it does when you stop asking it to hold you upright. [long pause] Your sacrum, that triangular bone at the base of the spine, pressing against the mattress. [long pause] The foundation of the foundation.

Your belly. [long pause] Soft. [long pause] Not held in. [long pause] Not braced. [long pause] Just soft and round and breathing on its own. [long pause] Your lower ribs expanding gently with each inhale, contracting with each exhale. [long pause] Your chest, rising and falling. [long pause] The sternum. [long pause] The collarbones. [long pause] The hollow of the throat.

Your shoulders. [long pause] Let them drop now. [long pause] They're almost certainly higher than you think. [long pause] There's always another quarter inch they can fall. [long pause] Let them fall it. [long pause] Feel the distance between your ears and your shoulders increase. [long pause] That space is freedom. [long pause] That space is the day releasing its grip on you.

Your arms, from shoulders to elbows. [long pause] The heavy bones of the upper arms. [long pause] Your elbows, those knobby hinges. [long pause] Your forearms, your wrists, the delicate machinery of your hands. [long pause] Your fingers, curled or open, resting on the blanket or at your sides. [long pause] Done gripping. [long pause] Done typing. [long pause] Done reaching. [long pause] Done.

Your neck. [long pause] The vertebrae stacked carefully, each one a small miracle of engineering. [long pause] Let the muscles along the sides of your neck soften. [long pause] The tendons at the back, releasing. [long pause] Your throat, open and unguarded.

Your jaw. [long pause] Let your teeth part. [long pause] Let the hinge of the jaw loosen so your mouth falls open just slightly. [long pause] Your tongue, resting loose at the bottom of your mouth. [long pause] Your lips, soft. [long pause] Your cheeks. [long pause] The muscles around your eyes... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] these tiny muscles that work all day, focusing and squinting and expressing. [long pause] Let them go slack. [long pause] Your eyelids, heavy, resting closed. [long pause] Your forehead, smoothing. [long pause] The space between your eyebrows, wide open. [long pause] The top of your head. [long pause] The back of your skull against the pillow.

The whole body now. [long pause] One continuous field of sensation, lying here, breathing. [long pause] You've visited every part. [long pause] You've given each region permission to stop. [long pause] The body has been acknowledged. [long pause] It can rest. [long pause] And now we turn to something else.

Your breathing. [long pause] In through the nose... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five. [long pause] And out, slowly, through the mouth... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] seven. [long pause] The exhale like a long ribbon of silk being drawn out, thinner and thinner until it dissolves.

Again. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] filling the lungs gently, not forcing... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and out, letting the breath thin out at the end until it's barely there.

Once more. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and out. [long pause] And now let the breath go. [long pause] Let it breathe you. [long pause] You are no longer in charge of it. [long pause] It continues on its own, quieter now, shallower, the breath of someone who is already nearly asleep.
"Be still, and know that I am God." Psalm forty-six, verse ten.

This verse comes from a psalm about chaos. [long pause] The nations rage. [long pause] The kingdoms totter. [long pause] The earth melts. [long pause] It is not a psalm about a peaceful afternoon. [long pause] It is a psalm about a world falling apart, and in the middle of the noise and the destruction, a voice says... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] be still. [long pause] Stop. [long pause] The command is given not to the chaos but to the listener. [long pause] You cannot stop the world from shaking. [long pause] But you can stop your own frantic response to it. [long pause] Be still. [long pause] And in the stillness... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] know.

We're going to sit with this verse the way the contemplative tradition has done for centuries. [long pause] Peeling it back, word by word, layer by layer. [long pause] Each time, we remove a word. [long pause] Each time, we go deeper. [long pause] Don't rush through this. [long pause] Let each version land. [long pause] Let it change something in you before we move to the next.
Be still... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and know that I am God.
Be still... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and know that I am.
Be still... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and know.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

What remains when all the words fall away? [long pause] [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] being. [long pause] Just presence. [long pause] You don't have to know anything tonight. [long pause] You don't have to believe anything with particular force. [long pause] You don't have to understand the theology or feel the right feelings. [long pause] You can simply... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] be. [long pause] And in the being, something is known that can't be reached by thinking.

Your mind is still active. [long pause] Of course it is. [long pause] That's what minds do. [long pause] They produce thoughts the way the heart produces beats. [long pause] You can't stop it, and the attempt to stop it only creates another thought: "I can't stop thinking." So instead of fighting your thoughts, we're going to practice something gentler. [long pause] We notice the thought. [long pause] We give it a quiet label... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] just one word. [long pause] And then we let it pass. [long pause] Like clouds crossing a sky at night. [long pause] The sky does not hold onto them. [long pause] The sky does not chase them. [long pause] It simply lets them move through.
When a thought comes, give it a label.
A thought about tomorrow... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] planning.
A memory from today... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] remembering.
A worry about something you can't control... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] worrying.
A judgment about how you're doing at this very practice... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] judging.
A sensation in your body that pulls your attention... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] feeling.
An urge to move or adjust or check your phone... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] wanting.
An emotion that surfaces without an obvious cause... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] feeling.

The label doesn't have to be perfect. [long pause] It's not the point. [long pause] The point is the gap... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] that tiny space between the thought arising and you noticing it. [long pause] That gap, however small, is the stillness. [long pause] It's the still point the psalm is pointing to. [long pause] It was there all along, underneath the noise.

Try it now. [long pause] Sit in the quiet. [long pause] Whatever comes, note it silently. [long pause] One word. [long pause] And then return to the silence. [long pause] Don't follow the thought. [long pause] Don't argue with it. [long pause] Don't build on it. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] label, and return.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

If you've been carried away by a thought... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] swept into a story, a plan, a worry, a memory... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and then suddenly you realize it happened... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] that moment of realization is not failure. [long pause] That moment is the practice. [long pause] That is the stillness breaking through. [long pause] You didn't fail. [long pause] You woke up. [long pause] Gently note the thought — thinking — and return to the quiet.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

Continue. [long pause] Thoughts arise like bubbles in still water. [long pause] You note them. [long pause] They pop. [long pause] The water is still again. [long pause] And then another bubble. [long pause] And you note it. [long pause] And it pops. [long pause] And the water is still.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

Good. [long pause] Now let the noting go. [long pause] You've practiced enough. [long pause] The mind is quieter now... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] maybe not silent, but the spaces between thoughts are longer. [long pause] The volume is lower. [long pause] The urgency is gone.

In this quieter place, we're going to rest in a single word. [long pause] This is centering prayer, an ancient practice from the Christian contemplative tradition. [long pause] You choose one word... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a word that draws you toward the presence of God... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and you hold it lightly. [long pause] Not gripping it. [long pause] Not repeating it with effort. [long pause] Just letting it rest in your awareness, the way a stone rests at the bottom of a clear lake.

Choose your word now. [long pause] It might come immediately. [long pause] Peace. [long pause] Rest. [long pause] Grace. [long pause] Love. [long pause] Mercy. [long pause] Father. [long pause] Jesus. [long pause] Abide. [long pause] Enough. [long pause] Silence.
Whatever word arrived first... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] trust it. [long pause] Don't second-guess it. [long pause] That's the one.
Now, silently, let the word drop into the quiet. [long pause] You don't have to think it loudly. [long pause] Just let it be there, like a sound heard from far away. [long pause] Your word.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

When your mind drifts... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and it will, because that's what minds do... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] gently return to the word. [long pause] Not with frustration. [long pause] Not with effort. [long pause] Like coming home after being out. [long pause] Oh... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] I was somewhere else. [long pause] Now I'm back. [long pause] That's all. [long pause] A gentle return.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

Let the spaces between the word grow longer. [long pause] The word... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] then silence... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] then, when you need it, the word again... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] then longer silence. [long pause] The silence is not empty. [long pause] It is full. [long pause] It is full of the presence you are resting in, whether you feel it or not. [long pause] The feeling is not the point. [long pause] The resting is.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

One more time. [long pause] Let the word appear. [long pause] And then... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] let even the word dissolve. [long pause] Let it sink to the bottom and disappear. [long pause] What remains is the silence it pointed to. [long pause] The presence it was a door into. [long pause] You're through the door now. [long pause] You don't need the word. [long pause] You are in the room.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
"In peace I will lie down and sleep, for you alone, Lord, make me dwell in safety." Psalm four, verse eight.

You don't need to make yourself safe tonight. [long pause] You don't need to solve the unsolvable. [long pause] You don't need to plan your way to peace. [long pause] You don't need to control what happens while you sleep or what waits for you in the morning. [long pause] You are made to dwell in safety... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] not by your own effort, but by the One who holds the dark hours as easily as the light. [long pause] The night is His too. [long pause] He does not sleep. [long pause] He is not worried about tomorrow. [long pause] You can borrow His peace for the next eight hours. [long pause] He is offering it freely.

Lord... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] we have tried to be still tonight. [long pause] Our minds wandered and we brought them back. [long pause] They wandered again and we brought them back again. [long pause] This is our offering... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] not perfection but presence. [long pause] Imperfect, distracted, honest presence. [long pause] We showed up. [long pause] Meet us in the quiet... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] in the gaps between the thoughts... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] in the space where the word used to be. [long pause] That's where you've always been. [long pause] We know that now. [long pause] Or we're beginning to. [long pause] Amen.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

Be still. [long pause] The world turns without your attention tonight. [long pause] The morning will come without your effort. [long pause] The people you love are held by the same God who holds you. [long pause] Nothing needs you right now except the pillow and the quiet and the dark.
Just being. [long pause] Just breathing. [long pause] Just resting in the word... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] or in the silence where the word used to be.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

Still... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] quiet... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] safe. [long pause] The thoughts thin out like stars fading at dawn, except it's the other direction... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] you're fading into the dark, which is where sleep lives, which is where rest has always been waiting.
The stillness deepens. [long pause] The body is heavy. [long pause] The breath is barely there... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] soft and shallow and slow.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
